draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer navigation split between persistent sidebar (core functions) and tab groups (contextual workspaces)
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation model: sidebar-dominant vs tab-dominant vs hybrid
facade.label: Sidebar with tabs
facade.acceptImplies: Explore how sidebar scales to hold 8+ tool categories while tabs manage workspace switching
facade.rejectImplies: Test pure tab navigation or full-screen modal-based tool switching as alternative

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer navigation split between persistent sidebar (core functions) and tab groups (contextual workspaces)
evidence.implication: Explore how sidebar scales to hold 8+ tool categories while tabs manage workspace switching

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI tools prefer high-contrast, distraction-free interfaces with generous whitespace and dark color schemes
facade.axisTargeted: Light vs Dark + Density (sparse vs dense)
facade.label: Dark minimal workspace
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into dark mode, minimal chrome, breathing room around elements
facade.rejectImplies: Explore brighter palettes or more compact, information-dense layouts

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants bright energetic workspace vs calm focused environment for concentration
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple summaries and key metrics only
Whether user wants instant responsive feedback vs smooth gradual transitions and animations

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants bright energetic workspace vs calm focused environment for concentration
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple summaries and key metrics only
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant responsive feedback vs smooth gradual transitions and animations
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users working in AI workspaces prefer fluid, continuous scrolling and panning over jarring, instant jumps between content areas.
facade.label: Smooth scroll
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize easing curves, momentum scrolling, and continuous gestures; treat viewport navigation as a dance, not a teleport.
facade.rejectImplies: Embrace instant loading states, sharp CSS transitions, and click-to-load patterns; motion is overhead in favor of immediate feedback.

facade.hypothesis: AI workspaces benefit from warm, ambient lighting that feels human-scaled rather than sterile or clinical
facade.label: Soft, warm light
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward interfaces with warm color temps, soft shadows, and inviting visual comfort over stark minimalism
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers cool, bright, or neutral palettes; values clarity and edge over coziness; may lean clinical or tech-forward aesthetic

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer a friendly, approachable tone over corporate formality
facade.axisTargeted: voice and personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Casual and conversational
facade.acceptImplies: The product should use plain language, warm microcopy, and conversational UI patterns to feel like a helpful colleague rather than enterprise software
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer a more polished, professional, or authoritative voice that signals competence and reliability over warmth

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer scanning individual metrics in isolated visual units rather than reading dense data tables
facade.label: Numbers in cards
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should use card-based layouts with one metric per card, supporting quick status checks without context-switching
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace should favor unified tables, dashboards, or narrative text that keeps related data together even if it requires more reading

facade.hypothesis: Users want all tool categories visible simultaneously in a compact sidebar rather than hidden behind dropdowns, accepting occasional overflow in exchange for discovery
facade.axisTargeted: Sidebar density and information architecture (dense/collapsed toggle vs. always-expanded)
facade.label: Dense sidebar with toggles
facade.acceptImplies: Explore wide sidebars (200px+) with icon+label pairs, category sections with toggle-able tool groups, and how users scan vs. drill patterns change
facade.rejectImplies: Test narrow sidebars (60-80px icon-only), with tools hidden in dropdowns or secondary panels, focusing interactions on 'search first' vs. 'browse'

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and intuitive navigation.
